name: Rich Cocoa Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter an 8- or 9-inch square pan and set aside.
2. In a heavy, 4-quart saucepan, combine sugar, cocoa, and salt. Stir in milk.
3. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture reaches a full rolling boil.
4. Boil without stirring to 234°F (soft-ball stage) or until the syrup forms a soft ball when dropped into very cold water (the bulb of the candy thermometer should not rest on the bottom of the saucepan).
5. Remove from heat and stir in butter and vanilla.
6. Cool at room temperature until the mixture reaches 110°F (lukewarm).
7. Beat with a wooden spoon until the fudge thickens and loses some of its gloss.
8. Quickly spread into the prepared pan.
9. Cut into squares.
